description: Cross-referenced BB defense ranges for 6-max cash games (100bb, standard rake). Defense frequencies and hand selection for calling and 3-betting from the big blind vs each position's open raise. Key verified data: BB vs BTN = ~51.7% defense range (text-extracted from Upswing published article).

sources.name: Upswing Poker
sections: BB defense strategy guide for cash games
Educa-p0ker Elite Cash Game Mastery chart reference
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — key verified stat: '51.7% of hands' defense range BB vs BTN (2.5bb open, $1/$2 cash). Confirms: K7o, Q8o, 84s are FOLDS vs BTN despite seeming strong. 53o is a profitable call. Charts are image-based behind paid course.
verified_facts: BB vs BTN defense = ~51.7% (2.5bb open, standard online rake)
53o → profitable CALL vs BTN (article explicitly states this)
BB has position advantage postflop → defend wider than MDF alone suggests

sources.name: Upswing Poker 6-Max Guide
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — confirms SB should lean 3-bet-or-fold vs raises; confirms BB defends wide. Charts are Upswing Lab images.
verified_facts: SB vs raise: lean towards 3-betting entire continue range (to mitigate positional disadvantage)
BB: should always defend against BTN steals — has odds and position

sources.name: GTO Wizard Blog
sections: BB response to SB open — polarized range example with specific hands
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — Confirms BB vs SB response is polarized: strong hands + trash bluffs (T2s, K5o mentioned as bluffs in this spot). Medium hands (A9s, 88) are CALLS.
verified_facts: BB vs SB: polarized 3-bet range (strong hands + trash/bluffs)
A9s, 88 = CALLS not 3-bets in BB vs SB
T2s, K5o = bluff 3-bets in BB vs SB (at the edge of the call threshold)

sources.name: PokerCoaching.com (Jonathan Little)
sections: Facing RFI: Big Blind — BB vs UTG/UTG+1, UTG+2, LJ, HJ, CO, BTN, SB scenarios
sources.data_quality: PDF DOWNLOADED — all BB defense scenarios confirmed as separate charts (7 distinct matchups). Hand grids are image-based. Sizing conventions: 2.5x open, 3x 3-bet in position.

crossReference.agreementLevel: all 3 agree on framework; 2 of 3 (Upswing + GTO Wizard) provide text-verifiable specific hands/stats
crossReference.notes: All sources agree on defense frequency trend (tighter vs EP, wider vs LP). Upswing provides verified defense percentage (51.7% vs BTN). GTO Wizard confirms polarized construction for 3-bet component. PokerCoaching confirms all 7 BB defense scenarios exist. Minor differences in exact hand lists reflect different rake/stack assumptions but principles are identical.

discrepancy_analysis.bluff_hands: Upswing (IP postflop): uses A7o, A6o, A2o, J2s, T3s as bluffs BB vs BTN. GTO Wizard: T2s, K5o as bluffs BB vs SB. These are DIFFERENT spots (BTN vs SB open) — not a contradiction.
discrepancy_analysis.fold_hands: Upswing explicitly confirms K7o, Q8o, 84s fold BB vs BTN — these look foldable but are. Useful for calibration.

assumptions: 100bb effective stacks, 6-max cash game, 2.5bb open raise size standard, no ante, standard online rake.

scenarios.label: BB vs UTG RFI (6max)
scenarios.rationale: UTG range ~17.6% (GTO Wizard confirmed). BB pot odds = 2.5x open → paying 1.5bb to win 4bb = ~27% equity needed. But most hands need to beat a 17.6% range, so defense tightens significantly.

actions.notes: Tightest BB defense spot in 6-max. UTG opens very strong range. Small pocket pairs are set-mines (profitable at 2.5bb open odds). Most weak suited hands fold.

scenarios.label: BB vs HJ RFI (6max)
scenarios.rationale: HJ range ~21.4% (wider than UTG). Defend slightly wider. Add K9s, some weaker suited hands.

actions.notes: Add K9s, Q9s, J9s, T8s to call range vs UTG defend. 99 and KQs join 3-bet value range.

scenarios.label: BB vs CO RFI (6max)
scenarios.rationale: CO range ~27.8% — many weaker hands included. Defend wider: more suited one-gappers, additional offsuit aces.

scenarios.label: BB vs BTN RFI (6max)
scenarios.verified_source_stat: Upswing Poker text-extracted: 'you still get to defend quite a bit in cash games (51.7% of hands, in this example)' — 2.5bb BTN open, $1/$2 cash
scenarios.rationale: BTN opens ~43.5% in 6-max. BB has pot odds + position postflop. Very wide defense justified. BTN range is heavily weighted toward speculative hands BB dominates.

actions.notes: Widest BB defense spot. 53o is profitable per Upswing. K7o, Q8o, 84s are folds per Upswing (confirmed). These hands look foldable but serve as calibration anchor.

scenarios.label: BB vs SB RFI (6max)
scenarios.rationale: SB opens to steal with very wide range (often 40-50%+ in aggressive games). BB has position postflop and SB is OOP. 3-bet-heavy strategy recommended (per Upswing: BB should 3-bet SB aggressively).

actions.notes: GTO Wizard blog text-extracted: A9s and 88 are CALLS (not 3-bets) in BB vs SB. T2s and K5o are bluff 3-bets (at the edge of call threshold). Polarized construction — medium hands prefer calling.
